title: Интернирование: пул из одной ссылки на значение

cards.prompt: The intern pool holds one reference per unique value: two equal literals share a reference (True), a runtime-built copy is a distinct object (False), and String.Intern returns the pooled reference so it equals the literal (True), printing True False True
cards.code: using System; string a = "hello world"; string b = "hello world"; string c = new string("hello world".ToCharArray()); Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(a, b)); Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(a, c)); Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(a, string.Intern(c)));

cards.prompt: String.IsInterned only probes the pool: a literal is found so IsInterned is non-null (True), a value built at runtime is not in the pool so IsInterned is null (True), and built is 1000-runtime, printing True True 1000-runtime
cards.code: using System; string lit = "interned-yes"; string built = 1000 + "-runtime"; Console.WriteLine(string.IsInterned(lit) != null); Console.WriteLine(string.IsInterned(built) == null); Console.WriteLine(built);

cards.prompt: A runtime-built string with an equal value is a different object (False), but String.Intern returns the pooled reference equal to the literal (True), printing False True
cards.code: using System; string x = "abc123"; string y = new string(new[]{'a','b','c','1','2','3'}); Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(x, y)); string z = string.Intern(y); Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(x, z));
